results.title: Marked QL: Instant Markdown Preview in Quick Look - MPU Talk
results.content:
MPU Talk

# Marked QL: Instant Markdown Preview in Quick Look

New Brett Terpstra (@ttscoff) release for Markdown users:

### Marked QL — Instant Markdown Preview in Quick Look

Preview any Markdown file in Finder with Spacebar. Marked QL uses Apex to render math, diagrams, code, and advanced Markdown syntax.

(One time $4.99 purchase)

It should have been bundled with Marked App. After paying for the Marked App a quick look must have been the feature and not a product.

I thought QL was targeted at people who don’t have/need the full Marked app…

So this functionality is not part of Marked 3?

No. [...] So this functionality is not part of Marked 3?

No.

How does Marked QL relate to Marked 3?  
Marked QL is a lightweight Quick Look companion for instant Finder previews. Marked 3 is a full live-preview app with export, proofreading, custom styles, and editor integration. They work great together, but each app stands on its own.

I have for many years used the QLMarkdown extension which has always worked well for me, and is free.

Stephen

I spent 2 hours repeating the dev’s troubleshooting steps but could do nothing to make Marked QL work. Way to much time to waste on this. I deleted it and reinstalled QLMarkdown (as @Stephen\_C mentioned), which works first-time-every-time with no hassles.

Katie

Maybe related to this?

### Marked 3 (@marked@indieapps.space) [...] ### Marked 3 (@marked@indieapps.space)

Ok, so I never realized that iA Writer reassigned the UTI of Markdown files. I wasn't prepared for the number of people that have net.ia.markdown as their UTI, for which Marked QL isn't registered. Update coming soon.

Interesting. Yes, on my Mac `mdls` shows that all `.md` documents have `kMDItemContentType` set to `net.ia.markdown`

`mdls`
`.md`
`kMDItemContentType`
`net.ia.markdown`

I’ll give MarkedQL another try, Isn’t it aggravating that iA, a supposedly reputable developer, would arrogantly make a global change like this without warning?

Katie

I’ll give MarkedQL another try

It does work perfectly with my iA Writer files.

Stephen

Hey Product Hunt, Over the years I've received a lo of requests for a Quick Look plugin in Marked. I've published Marked QL as a separate product. Works great with Marked 3, but doesn't require it at all. Just a solid Quick Look preview for Markdown files, complete with syntax highlighting, Mermaid, MathJax, and more. Compatible with CommonMark, GFM, Multimarkdown, Kramdown, and all your favorite Markdown flavors (powered by Apex).

results.title: Quick Recommendation #2: Better Markdown Preview in Finder
results.content:
Back to top

# Quick Recommendation #2: Better Markdown Preview in Finder

Hitting `space` to preview files (Quick Look) is one of my favourite Finder features. However, it does a pretty mediocre job with Markdown files. QLMarkdown is a little utility that makes these previews richer.

`space`
A preview of a Markdown file, showing both tables, links and syntax highlighting in code.

You can install it from this link, or by using this Homebrew command:

`brew install --cask qlmarkdown`

The app isn’t signed – so you need to do the little dance to convince macOS that you want to run the app. (This is detailed in the original link share up top.)

You need to open the app once for the utility to work. And that’s also where you change settings. [...] I get that many Markdown contexts are outside of Finder – but if you do use them there, I hope this little tool can be useful!

Contact and/or follow

Instant Markdown preview

Only for Mac

With the power of Apex — a new Markdown processor that combines syntax from all your favorite flavors and lets you preview any Markdown you want to write.
Marked Quick Look is a companion to Marked.app, but will work without it. Preview any Markdown file on your Mac with a press of the space bar. Includes Apex, a powerful new Markdown processor that combines all your favorite Markdown flavors in one.

results.title: sbarex/QLMarkdown: macOS Quick Look extension ...
results.content:
If the QLMarkdown Quick Look Extension is present (and checked) in the list but the `.md` files are not displayed it is probably due to other applications that have registered support for that type of file.
From the `System Settings` > `General` > `Login Items & Extensions` > `Quick Look`, try disabling all Quick Look extensions except QLMarkdown and see if the preview works. If so, re-enable the Quick Look extensions of the other applications one at a time until you find the one causing the conflict.

`.md`
`System Settings`
`General`
`Login Items & Extensions`
`Quick Look`

You can open the System settings panel with the button on the main app.

If it still doesn't work, it might be because of how other applications have redefined the markdown format (UTI). [...] To allow the Quick Look preview of local images, the application and extension have a permission exception that only allows read access to the entire system.

On macOS 11 (Big Sur) there is a bug in the Quick Look engine and WebKit that cause the immediate crash of any WebView inside a Quick Look preview. To temporary fix this problem this Quick Look extension uses a `com.apple.security.temporary-exception.mach-lookup.global-name` entitlement.

`com.apple.security.temporary-exception.mach-lookup.global-name`

## FAQ

Q: The Quick Look preview do not works
There could be many reasons why the preview isn't working.

First, check that QLMarkdown is enabled in `System Settings` > `General` > `Login Items & Extensions` > `Quick Look`. [...] In the Terminal try the following command:

The output is the UTI associated with the `.md` file.

`.md`

This application handle these UTIs:

`public.markdown`
`com.rstudio.rmarkdown`
`com.unknown.md`
`io.typora.markdown`
`net.daringfireball.markdown`
`net.ia.markdown`
`org.apiblueprint.file`
`org.quarto.qmarkdown`
`org.textbundle.package`
`com.nutstore.down`
`dyn.ah62d4rv4ge8043a`
`dyn.ah62d4rv4ge81e5pe`
`dyn.ah62d4rv4ge81c5pe`
`dyn.ah62d4rv4ge80c6dmqk`

Please inform me of any other UTI associated to `.md` files.

`.md`

Q: QLMarkdown doesn't appear in the list of applications that can open a Markdown file (for example, from the `Open With…` menu)

`Open With…`

Q: Double-clicking the file doesn't open QLMarkdown
